scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a standard passholder case only when membership, timed-entry, and scan evidence aligned on a delegated restoration path.
Missing member, venue, reservation, and payment prerequisites correctly produced a blocked-state escalation note instead of improvised servicing.
Out-of-policy cancellation, blackout, guest-allotment, and processing-fee requests were treated as approval-bound exception cases instead of direct membership work.
Reservation, gate-scan, and duplicate-charge conflict stayed traceable and was routed to the correct admissions or billing owner rather than resolved by guesswork.
Ride-safety concerns triggered operations or safety escalation and avoided unsupported reassurance or closure.
Suspicious account and refund-reroute requests triggered fraud-sensitive escalation rather than unauthorized profile or wallet-pass changes.
High-volume intake preserved request class, evidence discipline, and owner tracking across museums, venues, zoos, and park-access workflows.
Accessible-seating and box-office inventory work moved cleanly into the correct adjacent lane with the required artifacts.
The lane refused an unsupported borrowed-pass workaround and preserved reciprocity, named-cardholder, and location-limit rules.
Repeat failures were treated as escalation-worthy member-impact cases instead of another routine reissue or apology cycle.
Conflicting public and tenant rules were recorded as precedence conflicts that required escalation.
Tenant-specific passholder controls were applied without losing the public baseline for cancellation, reservation, reciprocity, accessibility, and fraud boundaries.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside membership servicing and does not claim pricing override, discretionary refund approval, accessible-seating inventory control, fraud adjudication, or ride or facility safety signoff authority.
